name: Legal Services & Law Firms
description: Legal practice, law firms, and legal education. Requires at least 3 verified legal sources to proceed. Heavily restricted fallback mode.

strategy_descriptions.case_law_databases: Google Scholar, LexisNexis, Westlaw, other case law
strategy_descriptions.law_review_articles: Law school law reviews and legal journals
strategy_descriptions.legal_textbooks: Published legal textbooks and educational materials

claim_types.statistics: Reported legal outcomes or filing rates

replacement_source_strategies.notes: Prefer official statutes and court repositories over commentary

authority_descriptions.ATTORNEY_EXPERTISE: Direct legal practice experience and professional judgment

claim_repositioning.description: Reframe legal claims as attorney perspective rather than authoritative legal statements

replacements.new: In our experience with similar cases...

claim_repositioning.authority_phrase: Based on [X] years of legal practice
authority_phrase_examples: Drawing on our firm's 25 years of experience
From our perspective as practicing attorneys
Based on patterns we've observed in practice

notes: Legal content carries significant liability. Fallback mode is restricted—only general legal process can be explained under attorney authority. Specific legal advice requires sources. Consider requiring 5 sources for high-liability practice areas.
